transcript: Speaker A: I left my village and came to this city. Leaving my father. My mother. My home behind. People said I won't succeed. They said, 'English is not for someone like you.' But I refused to believe them. Because I carry something they can't see. My father's sacrifice. My mother's prayers. Their tired hands. Their sleepless nights. All of it... for me. Some days, the city feels too loud. The language feels too foreign. I miss home. I miss them. But then I remember why I left. I open MySivi. Just 10 minutes. The AI teacher doesn't judge me. Every mistake? A lesson. Every session? One step closer to them. I'm not competing with this city. I'm just better than I was yesterday. When doubt creeps in, I open the app. When loneliness hits at midnight, I press start. Because I made a promise — to them. One day soon, I'll call my father with confidence. I'll walk into that interview room with my head high. I'll send money home. I'll make them proud. Not because I was the smartest. Not because it was easy. But because I didn't give up. MySivi didn't just teach me English. It reminded me who I'm doing this for. Mummy, Papa... Your son is still trying. And your son is not done yet. I will practice on MySivi everyday and make you proud. Speaker B: इंस्टॉल नाउ पर क्लिक करो और मायसीवी ऐप डाउनलोड करो। [Click on install now and download the MySivi app.]

summary: A young man narrates his emotional journey of moving to the city and learning English using the MySivi app to honor his parents' sacrifices. He highlights his written story on paper, emphasizing daily practice and determination. The ad concludes with a call to download the app.
